profile.name: GeunHyeong Kim
profile.title: Robotics Software Engineer
profile.about_quote: I am a robotics engineer with a focus on perception (vision), SLAM, and Navigation for legged robotic systems.

featured_experience.title: 로빛 (RO:BIT) - 광운대학교 로봇게임단
featured_experience.description: 19th Humanoid Team Member. Leading the development of vision-based navigation and autonomous localization for humanoid robots.
highlights: Development of vision-based navigation and object recognition for humanoid robots
Implementation of localization algorithms for autonomous robot movement
Participation in various national and international robot competitions

competition_awards.title: 제10회 대한민국 SW 융합 해커톤
competition_awards.description: Led the team for social problem solving. Developed the frontend platform for innovative software solutions.

competition_awards.title: 광운대학교 공학설계입문 작품전시회
competition_awards.description: Object detection using Google Teachable Machine, intuitive web UI design, and precise motor control systems.

competition_awards.title: 로보컵 코리아 오픈 2025
competition_awards.description: Humanoid Kid Size league. Developed field perception and robot motion control for competitive robotic soccer.

competition_awards.title: 2025 지능로봇대회 휴머노이드 지능형 변형 바이애슬론
competition_awards.description: Intelligent Biathlon category. Implemented vision-based perception and adaptive motion control for complex environments.

competition_awards.title: 로보컵 코리아 오픈 2026
competition_awards.description: Humanoid Kid Size league. Advanced vision, localization, and motion control optimization for world-class performance.

general_projects.title: Robotics Research Project
general_projects.description: Space for personal research and development projects for robot autonomous navigation and mapping.

details: Supported conference operations and technical sessions for the ROS community
Engaged with robotics developers and researchers to facilitate event workflows
Technical mentoring and instruction for problem-solving hackathon participants
Guided students in robotics and software development methodologies
Managed competition operations and technical support for robot games
Coordinated field setups and ensured smooth competition workflows
Member of Robot Game Team RO:BIT
Focused on Vision, Localization, and Humanoid Robotics
